hero.summary: I lead data strategy, governance, and cloud platforms to accelerate decisions and create competitive advantage.

about.title: Who I am
about.subtitle: 10+ years turning data into decisions that move businesses forward.
about.body_1: I design and build modern data architectures that scale. Databricks specialist — my playground includes Delta Lake, Unity Catalog, Delta Live Tables, and MLflow. I apply rigorous governance, intelligent automation, and data quality in enterprise environments processing petabytes.
about.body_2: Before bronze, there's always a landing zone: I bring every business source into a single append-only lake — what I call the oil lake. Each row keeps its full change history, which makes auditing trivial and feeds data quality in the silver layer. Because it's raw and immutable, this zone can be recovered and reprocessed on any stack — the architecture is never held hostage by a single tool.
about.body_3: I lead cross-functional squads to deliver data-driven platforms that accelerate time-to-insight. In the gold layer, my bet is domain-oriented One Big Tables: instead of stalling the team defining entities and relationships, I deliver the right grain for each domain's analytics — so the Single Source of Truth gets adopted by default, not by mandate. Robust orchestration (Databricks Workflows, Airflow, dbt) and cloud-native on GCP and Azure, built for scale, performance, and optimized cost.
about.bullet_1: Domain-oriented One Big Tables in the gold layer — SSOT adopted without traditional entity-relationship modeling
about.bullet_2: End-to-end governance with Unity Catalog and automated lineage
about.bullet_3: High-performance teams with DevOps culture and data quality
about.bullet_4: Append-only landing zone (oil lake): per-row history for auditing and reprocessing on any stack
about.tagline: Architecture that scales in record time — and that teams adopt on their own.

arch.title: Living architecture
arch.subtitle: From the oil lake to domain-oriented OBTs — data flows, gets qualified, and turns into a decision.
arch.aria: Data-flow diagram: multiple sources land in the landing zone (oil lake, append-only), pass through bronze (pre-processing), through silver (enrichment, qualification, and a data-quality quarantine), and reach gold, where they become domain-oriented OBTs with facts, dimensions, and the grain of each view.
arch.s_bronze_desc: Raw ingestion · typing · no business rules
arch.s_gold_desc: Domain OBTs · facts + dimensions · grain per view
arch.legend_note: Each layer refines the previous one — reprocessable on any stack from the landing zone.

impact.title: Impact in numbers
impact.subtitle: Scalable data architecture, delivered and adopted in record time.
impact.metric_1_label: Tables in the medallion lakehouse (bronze → silver → gold), in 3 months
impact.metric_3_label: Engineers to build it all (1 mid-level + 1 senior)
impact.metric_4_label: Of query volume migrated to the new architecture in under 3 months
impact.highlight_items: GOLD as domain-oriented One Big Tables · SSOT adopted without the entity-relationship trap · record adoption at a 160-person company

aidev.title: AI-assisted development, governed
aidev.subtitle: The numbers above shipped in record time because AI here runs on architecture: specialized agents, guardrails, and versioned knowledge — not loose prompting.
aidev.stats_aria: Aggregate metrics of the AI harness ecosystem
aidev.pillar_orchestration_desc: An orchestrator breaks the demand down, dispatches specialists in isolated context, and a validator cross-checks everything against official docs. Each specialist returns a ≤250-word summary — lean context, predictable cost.
aidev.pillar_guardrails_desc: A denylist blocks production deploys, force-push, resetting main, and reading secrets; read-only allowlist by default. Agents propose and audit, but ask for confirmation before applying or opening a ticket.
aidev.pillar_context_desc: Hooks inject Git state, load the right skill by keyword, and validate YAML and Conventional Commits on every edit. Lean CLAUDE.md + on-demand loading via @path.
aidev.pillar_knowledge_desc: Domain KBs, ADRs, and a memory-keeper that fights knowledge staleness. The medallion layer contract (bronze → silver → gold) is enforced by CI tests.
aidev.cases_desc: The same architecture shows up outside work: this portfolio (art-director → motion-engineer → frontend-builder → a11y/performance audit pipeline) and saul_goodman (review agents, i18n across 13 locales, QA, and ML foundations).

enrich.title: Data enrichment machines
enrich.subtitle: I turn public web data into enriched, trustworthy datasets: a configurable scraping + LLM machine with deterministic identity resolution and guardrails against hallucination.
enrich.card_scrape_desc: requests + a headless reader proxy that bypasses JS and anti-bot, with BeautifulSoup for link discovery. Concurrent workers, polite rate-limiting, retry with mirror fallback, and content deduplicated and truncated to control token cost.
enrich.card_llm_desc: Structured JSON extraction at temperature 0 with "never invent" prompts; deterministic regex handles everything that doesn't need an LLM. The model is chosen in a 10-endpoint bake-off (cost × latency × accuracy), with automatic fallback and fields cross-checked before writing.

recommendations.title: Recommendations
recommendations.subtitle: Feedback from leaders and partners throughout the journey.

rafael_carvalho.text:
Otávio was a key player in building HeroSpark's data infrastructure. With top-tier technical expertise and a strong focus on best practices and standards, he helped lay the foundation that now ensures robust data analysis and strategic decision-making.

Beyond his deep knowledge of data architecture, what truly stands out about Otávio is his speed and willingness to help. He was always ready to collaborate, find efficient solutions, and ensure everything was structured for the company's long-term growth.

If you're looking for a highly skilled professional who delivers fast and with excellence, Otávio is the one! 🚀

allan_ribeiro.text: Proactivity, commitment, efficiency, and reliability are some of Otávio's many qualities. An exceptional professional who always sought to improve. Quick to solve problems and attentive to clarifying doubts. Not only that, but also a friendly person to live with in the daily work routine.

lucas_brandao.text: Otávio is the type of pro who really knows how to lead a team to new skills and challenges, all in the most motivated way possible. He's got a massive experience in the field, and he's fantastic at guiding and encouraging beginners like nobody else. Plus, he's really passionate about technology - you can see it in the authority he proves when coding or discussing multiple stacks.

fernando_viegas.text:
Otávio is an excellent professional. He helped me a lot at the beginning of my career in the Data field. He gave me confidence and helped me develop as a Data Engineer.

A guy who is very dedicated to the project and extremely trustworthy. Otávio directly participated in the creation of our Data team and helped create/promote the Data Driven culture in our company.

guilherme_maduro.text: Don is the typical partner who solves absolutely any problem. It's a huge relief to know that I can count on him working on a task because it's guaranteed that it will be solved, regardless of complexity.

jaquelyne_kelm.text: I worked with Otávio in the Customer Success area, where I was able to follow the improvements and continuous evolution implemented in the team's operations. An excellent professional, with good analytical skills and great knowledge in data manipulation. His commitment to work is unquestionable.

eleicoes.uf_lead: State-level polls by state (excludes the presidential/national race).
eleicoes.title: The election's measuring machine
eleicoes.description: A pipeline over the TSE's official dataset (2026 Electoral Polls): who is measuring the electorate, where, with what sampling rigor and at what cost. Registration data, non-partisan by design.
eleicoes.hero_title: Who is measuring Brazil before October 4th?
eleicoes.hero_lead: Every electoral poll must be registered with the TSE. This observatory reads that official registry and shows the election's measuring machine — who measures, where, with what sampling rigor and at what cost. No guess on who wins: the percentages aren't in the registry, only the metadata.
eleicoes.vote_lead: What the polls measure for the presidency — aggregated from institutes registered with the TSE (via Wikipedia). Every figure carries institute, date and margin. It's measurement, not prediction.
eleicoes.serie_aria: Vote intention over time in the 1st round, by candidate
eleicoes.eff_note_b: Measures volume per real declared to the TSE — not the accuracy or rigor of the polls.
eleicoes.vote_nota: Source: Wikipedia — aggregation of polls registered with the TSE. The leader highlight (in gold) is visual only, with no party color. v1 scope: presidency.
eleicoes.est_lead: Governor and senator by state — same aggregation of institutes (via Wikipedia), with institute, date and margin in view. Pick a state.
eleicoes.est_nota: Source: Wikipedia — state polls registered with the TSE. In 2026 each state renews 2 of its 3 Senate seats. No party color; the leader highlight is visual only. DF, MS and MT have no article at the source yet.
eleicoes.explorar_lead: Pick a state and see the local cut: poll volume, sampling rigor, and the pollsters most active there.
eleicoes.kpis_lead: Polls registered with the TSE for the 2026 election — Python · pandas · Parquet.
eleicoes.timeline_lead: Polls released per month. Intensity accelerates as the election approaches.
eleicoes.institutos_lead: Pollsters by number of registered polls and declared spend (R$).
eleicoes.como_lead: Level of the race, who commissioned it, and sampling rigor.
eleicoes.nota: Scope: the TSE's registration dataset — poll metadata, not the vote-intention percentages (which are in the PDF questionnaires).
eleicoes.elt_lead: The body pollsters try to measure — the real profile from Brazil's National Voter Registry (TSE), aggregated from 11 million per-precinct rows.
eleicoes.elt_nota: Source: TSE — Electorate Statistics (current snapshot). Optional voting: ages 16–17, 70+ and illiterate voters.
eleicoes.iuf_title: Where the race is actually hot
eleicoes.iuf_lead: Raw counts favor big states. Here: polls per million voters — crossing the TSE registry with the Voter Registry. It reveals where measurement is most intense, regardless of size.
eleicoes.iuf_nota: Index = registered state polls ÷ state voters (in millions). Excludes the national/presidential race.
eleicoes.jogo_lead: The seats in play and the public campaign money — the size of the 2026 bet.
eleicoes.rel_lead: The official milestones of the 2026 electoral calendar — from conventions to inauguration.
eleicoes.tr_title: How this panel handles vote intention
eleicoes.tr_lead: An honest slice of what public data allows — measurement, not prediction.
eleicoes.tr_have_text: Metadata for every poll registered with the TSE (institute, office, state, sample, cost, dates), the full demographic profile of the electorate, and presidential vote intention aggregated from institutes (via Wikipedia). All by a reproducible pipeline — Python · pandas · Parquet — with no LLM at runtime.
eleicoes.tr_havenot_text: Vote intention is always shown with institute, date and margin in view, and as an average of several polls — never a single number. We make no result projection and use no party color: the leader highlight is visual only. It's the measurement of the electorate, not a guess at who wins.

saul_goodman.title: Saul Goodman — Anti-Procrastination Extension
saul_goodman.description: Chrome extension that tracks tabs in real time and turns user behavior into a focus index. A deep learning model runs locally in the browser, learns usage patterns and recommends personalized adjustments — which villain sites to block, peak distraction hours and tailored focus goals. All in chrome.storage.local, no external servers.

pix_agent.welcome: Hi! I'm the PIX Observatory agent. Ask me anything about PIX data — growth, average ticket, availability, architecture.
pix_agent.note: Local demo · fixed keyword-based responses, no real LLM
pix_agent.aria_fab_open: Open chat with the PIX agent
pix_agent.aria_fab_close: Close chat with the PIX agent

responses.total: Based on BACEN data, PIX has processed **over 45 billion transactions** since November 2020. In 2024, R$ 4.4 trillion was moved — 106,000× the volume of the first day.
responses.ticket: The PIX average ticket is **R$ 17** day-to-day, but rises to **R$ 700+** during peaks. Reflects B2B adoption: companies move payments outside banking hours.
responses.growth: Historical growth: **106,000×** since day one. On 11/03/2020 there were 2,345 transactions. In 2025, peaks reach **258 million/day**. No payment system in the world has grown this fast.
responses.sla: PIX maintains **99.9%+ availability** historically, with average response time under 10s. Operates 24/7/365 — including holidays and late nights.
responses.architecture:
Medallion architecture:
**Bronze**: python-bcb → raw Parquet
**Silver**: DuckDB + Pandas → cleaning and typing
**Gold**: aggregations + KPIs

Orchestration via GitHub Actions (cron). This chat is a local keyword-based demo, no real LLM — GitHub Pages doesn't run a backend.
responses.users: PIX has **160M+ registered users**, **800+ participating institutions** and **700M+ keys** in DICT — more than 75% of Brazil's adult population.
responses.fallback: Based on BACEN data: PIX processes 258M+ transactions/day and moved R$ 4.4T in 2024. For "{query}", check the BACEN API directly via python-bcb.
